completion_criteria: Python direct operation executor prune is implemented, validated, and closed out honestly.

execution_run.what happened: Pruned direct generated list operations from generated operation executors while keeping their direct command modules and operation artifacts generated.
execution_run.scope touched: command package IR, command-generation renderer, generated memory/planning projections, generated proof, planning classification, and plan state
execution_run.changed surfaces: memory/planning operation executors no longer support direct list operation ids; memory executor no longer renders the dead payload.assemble runtime bridge
execution_run.validations run: generation check; generated package static proof; Python conformance; Docker Python conformance; planning doctor; targeted pytest suite; stale-reference sweep
execution_run.result for continuation: portable direct commands are routed only through direct generated command modules, reducing runtime delegate surface
execution_run.next step: commit/push this slice, then continue #892 with the next live runtime delegate blocker.

delegation_outcome_feedback.route skipped reason: Small contract pruning slice; no code behavior should change except removing unreachable operation executor support for commands already directly generated.
delegation_outcome_feedback.quality concern: Do not stop generating operation contracts for direct commands; only remove run_operation_ir support and dead payload.assemble runtime bridge.
delegation_outcome_feedback.decomposition adjustment: Prune dead portable direct-command runtime delegates before migrating remaining live memory/planning delegates.

proof_report.proof achieved now: direct list commands are no longer supported by run_operation_ir and memory payload.assemble bridge is gone
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run pytest tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py packages/memory/tests/test_packaging.py packages/planning/tests/test_packaging.py -q

intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: generated memory/planning command registries still route direct list commands, while generated operation executors no longer advertise those operation ids or payload.assemble bridge.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: dead operation-executor support for direct generated list commands pruned
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: #1005 filed for generated force-include classification drift
execution_summary.resume from: Continue #892 with the next live runtime delegate blocker; this dead-executor-prune slice is complete.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded dead delegate cleanup is complete, but #892 remains open because other generated command modules still import package runtime modules.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Generated command registries still include direct list operations; generated operation executors do not; static/conformance/Docker proof passed.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Generated operation executors regain direct list operation ids or memory payload.assemble runtime bridge.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
